race.name: StafettVasan
race.tagline: Ninety kilometers, five legs, one team — the world's largest ski relay, splitting the legendary Vasaloppet course among friends from Sälen to Mora.

vitals.registration: Online registration, sells out rapidly — team captain registers
vitals.aid_stations: Full Vasaloppet checkpoint infrastructure at each exchange zone
vitals.cutoff_time: Individual leg cutoffs enforced at exchange zones

climate.description: Late February conditions in Dalarna. Temperatures typically range from -15°C to 0°C. Each relay leg skier experiences different conditions as the day progresses — early legs ski in cold dawn air, later legs may face afternoon warming.
challenges: Wax selection varies by leg — morning legs face different conditions than afternoon
Waiting at exchange zones in cold can chill muscles
Team logistics — coordinating 5 skiers and transport to exchange points
Each leg is ~18km — demanding enough to require genuine fitness

course.primary: The full 90km Vasaloppet course from Sälen to Mora, divided into five relay legs of approximately 18km each. Exchange zones are located at traditional Vasaloppet checkpoints. Each leg has its own character — from the forested opening to the flat finish into Mora.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks on the Vasaloppet course
course.grooming: World-class Vasaloppet standard — classic tracks prepared for relay format
features: World's largest ski relay — 2,400+ teams of 5
Full Vasaloppet course split into 5 legs of ~18km
Exchange zones at historic Vasaloppet checkpoints
Classic technique — same format as the main Vasaloppet
Team format makes Vasaloppet accessible to a wider audience
Final leg anchor skier finishes on the Vasaloppet finishing straight in Mora

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Massive participation (12,000+ skiers) on the iconic Vasaloppet course. World-class infrastructure and organization. The relay format creates a uniquely social experience while maintaining the prestige of skiing the full Sälen-to-Mora route. Slightly lower competitive depth due to team format.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Score 79 places in TIER 2. Relay format reduces individual competitive depth and international draw compared to the main Vasaloppet.

history.summary: StafettVasan was launched in 2003 as part of the Vasaloppet organization's effort to make the legendary Sälen-to-Mora course accessible to more skiers. By splitting the 90km course into five relay legs, teams of friends, families, and colleagues can collectively experience the full Vasaloppet route without each individual needing the fitness for 90km. The concept was an immediate hit, and StafettVasan has grown to become the world's largest ski relay with over 2,400 teams and 12,000 participants annually.
notable_facts: World's largest ski relay with 2,400+ teams and 12,000+ participants
Founded in 2003 alongside Nattvasan during Vasaloppet Winter Week expansion
Makes the Vasaloppet course accessible to skiers who cannot race the full 90km
Exchange zones at historic checkpoints — Smågan, Mångsbodarna, Risberg, Evertsberg, Oxberg
The anchor leg skier crosses the same finish line as Vasaloppet champions
Teams range from elite club squads to corporate and family groups

youtube_data.search_query: StafettVasan cross country ski race Sälen to Mora

videos.title: SYND 6 3 78 VASA CROSS COUNTRY SKI RACE IN MORA, SWEDEN
videos.description:
(5 Mar 1978) Scenes of Vasa Cross Country Ski Race in Mora, Sweden: Jean-Paul Pierrat of France won the 53-mile (85 kilometres) Vasa cross-country ski race on Sunday (5 March). Pierrat, a 30 year old customs officer from Chamonix, France, upset a field of Nordic favourites to be the first Frenchman to win the gruelling race which commemorates the 1521 flight from Danish occupants of Gustav Vasa, later king of Sweden. The winner finished the race in 5 hours, 20 minutes and 12 seconds - more than two minutes ahead of Tommy Jonsson, a 21- year old Mora skier. Third, beaten by jonsson on the finish in Vasa Street in Mora town centre, was Matti Kuoski of Hogsby, Sweden, winner of the 1974 and 1976 races. This year's race had gathered a record number of participants - 11,596 skiers from 19 nations - and took place in poor weather conditions. Only sixty percent of the skiers managed to cross the finish line

videos.title: SYND 5 3 79 12000 SKIERS TAKE PART IN CROSS COUNTRY SKI RACE IN SALEN
videos.description:
(4 Mar 1979) More than 12,000 skiers set off early in the world's biggest mass sports event, the Vasaloppet cross-country ski race. This annual event takes competitors through the forests of Sweden, along an 85-kilometre course. It's not just a race for experts. Those taking part come from many countries, and they're of both sexes and all ages. There is a dramatic finish, with Sweden's Ola Hassis finishing just ahead of rivals from Finland and Norway.

(5 Mar 1973) Worlds longest, annual, cross country ski race, the Vasaloppet, covers 53 miles (85 kilometers) between the towns Mora And Salen, in Sweden

videos.title: Vasastafetten
videos.description:
Folkfest när tio löpare delar på sträckan mellan Sälen och Mora. 
Räkna med folkfest när hundratals lag med tio löpare i varje – föreningar, företag, familjer och kompisgäng – delar på den klassiska sträckan från Sälen till Mora. Vasastafetten är ett terränglopp både för elitlöpare och motionärer, sträckorna är mellan 4,5 km och 15 km. Loppet har tre klasser: dam, herr och motion.

https://www.vasaloppet.se/lopp/loplopp/vasastafetten/

Running fest where ten runners split the distance from Sälen to Mora.
Count on big running festivities when hundreds of teams of ten runners each – associations, companies, families and groups of friends – share the classic stretch from Sälen to Mora. Vasastafetten is a cross-country race for both elite and recreational runners, with sections ranging between 4.5 km and 15 km. The race has three classes: women, men and leisure.

https://www.vasaloppet.se/en/races/running-races/vasastafetten/

videos.title: SYND 7 3 77 'VASALOPPET' CROSS COUNTRY SKIING RACE WON BY RUSSIAN
videos.description:
(6 Mar 1977) Twelve thousand skiers from eighteen nations lined up in the Swedish village of Mora  for the Vasaloppet.  This year's Vasaloppet, the name given to the world's longest and most arduous cross-country ski race. has been won for the first time by a Russian competitor. Ivan Caranin covered the 85-kilometres around the Swedish township of Mora in a time of four hours thirty minutes and four seconds. he finished almost two minutes ahead of his nearest rival for victory.  The wreath was placed around the winner's shoulders by a local girl Christina Andersson, herself a cross-country skier.

videos.title: Vasaloppet
videos.description: Vasaloppet är världens största långlopp på skidor. Traditionen är lång och hundratusentals skidåkare har kämpat sig fram i Vasaloppsspåret sedan starten 1922. För att klara av utmaningen på 90 km krävs kontinuerlig träning och noggranna förberedelser. Åkstilen är klassisk och naturupplevelsen magisk. I Sverige följs loppet av två miljoner tv-tittare. Nu är det dags att anta utmaningen!

videos.title: Syskonlag i Stafettvasan
videos.description: Många olika sorters lag deltog i Stafettvasan i år. Ett av dem var ett syskonlag som vi här följer.
videos.curation_reason: Team perspective following siblings through the relay race experience.

videos.title: Stafettvasan
videos.description:
Fem deltagare delar på den 90 km långa sträckan från Sälen till Mora. Det spelar ingen roll om ni är ett företag eller ett kompisgäng, alla får uppleva samma feststämning och glädje. Även om många av sträckorna är förhållandevis korta, så krävs träning och bra förberedelser. Varför inte träna tillsammans i laget? Då kan ni lyfta varandra hela vägen fram mot mållinjen i Mora.

Delsträckornas längd:
1. Start i Sälen–Mångsbodarna 24 km
2. Mångsbodarna–Evertsberg   24 km
3. Evertsberg–Oxberg                 14 km
4. Oxberg– Hökberg                      9 km
5. Hökberg– Mål i Mora              19 km
